Inflation Term-Structure Signal
← Back to dashboard

Inflation Term-Structure Signal

Inflation term-structure: breakevens and real yields across the 5-10 year horizon.

As of 2036-10-01

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given a composite score of 0.251 driven by rising 5-year breakevens (Z_T5: 0.61) and stable 10-year breakevens (Z_T10: 0.13), this setup aligns with Range-biased price paths and Normal volatility, where the dominant risk is Trend continuation, not Regime shift (1).
  • The signal currently occupies a Neutral regime, having moved upward from a recent low of -0.168 in February 2026 (1).
  • Conviction Band: Medium; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Improving; Threshold Proximity: Moderate; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Composite scores between -0.75 and 0.75 are classified as Neutral, serving as a weak conditioning variable for benchmark macro exposures (1).
  • Rising composite values signify a transition toward reflation and easier financial conditions (1).
  • Weights are applied to 5-year breakevens (0.4), 10-year breakevens (0.3), and 10-year real TIPS yields (0.3) (1).
  • Latest observation date: April 30, 2026 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the 5-year breakeven inflation rate (T5YIE), which rose to 2.56% (Z_T5: 0.61), indicating firming short-term inflation compensation (1).
  • Real yields (DFII10) remain slightly above the rolling norm (Z_mReal: -0.11), providing a modest offset to rising breakevens (1).
  • Momentum is positive, with the composite rising for two consecutive months, though it remains below the Reflation threshold of 0.75 (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: An absolute composite move exceeding 0.75, triggering a regime shift to Reflation or Disinflation.

Scenario Balance:

  • Base case dominant: Persistence in Neutral regime as breakevens and real yields oscillate within historical bands.
  • Upside risk: A transition to Reflation triggered by a sharp rise in 5-year inflation expectations above recent Z-score peaks.
  • Downside risk: A shift toward Disinflation driven by a spike in 10-year real yields (DFII10) or collapsing breakevens.

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Cyclical (months) based on the monthly resampling and rolling 5-year historical logic (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: Medium; while the signal is Neutral, the positive trajectory provides a secondary tilt toward reflationary assets.

Macro Relevance:

  • Informs the pricing and liquidity dimension by measuring market-implied inflation compensation versus real financial tightening (1).
  • Economic mechanism: The current rising trend implies a gradual easing of real financial conditions through higher inflation expectations (1).
  • Cycle position: Not determined by methodology.
  • Interacts with commodity price trends to confirm if breakeven moves are supported by physical market demand (1).

Regime Context:

  • The Neutral regime has been persistent since May 2023, with no breaches of the 0.75 thresholds in the provided history (1).
  • Direction of change: Strengthening towards the Reflation boundary (1).

Model Limitations:

  • TIPS liquidity premia can distort breakevens during periods of market stress (1).
  • The signal is a relative measure due to its rolling window logic, rather than an absolute level indicator (1).

Data & References:

  • Inflation Term-Structure Signal (Latest: 2026-04-30) (1).
  • Influential datapoints: T5YIE (2.56) and DFII10 (1.93) (1).
  • External survey-based inflation expectations and commodity price indices would improve signal validation (1).

Inflation Term-Structure Chart

Inflation term-structure composite from 5y/10y breakevens and 10y real yields.
